page.title: WildcatSage Bot Commands
page.expandTip: Click any command row below to expand and see usage details.

ask.name: !ask / !sage
ask.description: Ask Sage a question. Uses search intelligently.

search.name: !search
search.description: Searches the web for information on a topic.

game.name: !game
game.description: Provides researched information about the game currently being played. Add "analyze" to use AI image recognition on the stream (uses Twitch thumbnail that updates every 5 minutes).

translate.name: !translate
translate.description: Manage automatic message translation for users.

help.name: !commands / !help
help.description: Shows where to find the list of available commands.

lurk.name: !lurk
lurk.description: Let the chat know you are lurking. Provide an optional reason for a custom send-off.

quote.name: !quote
quote.description: Add, view, or search quotes from the channel. Quotes are stored per-channel and can include an optional author attribution.

usage.idDesc: Shows quote by ID number, e.g. !quote 12
usage.lastDesc: Shows the most recently added quote
usage.searchDesc: Searches quotes by text or author, e.g. !quote parfait
usage.addDesc: Adds a new quote. Optional author after " - ", e.g. !quote add "hello world - parfait"

command.name: !command Mod
command.description: Create, edit, and manage custom commands for your channel. Custom commands can also be managed via the Web Dashboard.

usage.addAi: Creates a new AI-powered command — generates a unique response each time using the prompt
usage.show: Shows the raw response template and current settings
usage.optionsPerms: Set who can use the command: everyone, subscriber, vip, moderator, or broadcaster
usage.optionsCooldown: Set a cooldown between uses (in seconds)
usage.optionsType: Switch an existing command between text mode and AI mode

geo.name: !geo
geo.description: Starts or manages Geo-Game, a geography guessing game for IRL or virtual worlds.

usage.region: Starts a region-specific real-world game, e.g. !geo Japan
usage.game: Starts a game-world game using current stream's game
usage.gameTitle: Starts a game-world game with specified game, e.g. !geo game "Metroid Prime"
usage.rounds: Runs any game command with N rounds, e.g. !geo Japan 3
usage.stop: Game initiator, mods, or broadcaster can stop
usage.interval: Sets time between clues to 30-300 seconds
usage.configRegion: Sets regions like Europe,Asia or USA,Canada

riddle.name: !riddle
riddle.description: Starts or manages a Riddle game. Riddles can be general, about the current stream game, or a specific subject.

usage.subject: Example: !riddle animals
usage.game: Riddle about the current stream game
usage.stop: Game initiator, mods, or broadcaster can stop
usage.questionTime: Sets time per riddle to 15-120 seconds
usage.keywordsLimit: Sets recent keywords to exclude, e.g. 20-100

trivia.name: !trivia
trivia.description: Starts or manages Trivia games with factual questions on various topics, including the current stream game.

usage.game: Starts trivia about the current stream game
usage.stop: Game initiator, mods, or broadcaster can stop
usage.time: Sets time per question to 10-120 seconds

botlang.name: !botlang
botlang.description: Configure the bot to speak in a specific language for the entire channel.

usage.status: Shows current auto-chat mode, frequency, and categories
usage.freq: Sets minimum gap between auto messages (minutes)

disable.name: !disable
disable.description: Disables a command in this channel.

enable.name: !enable
enable.description: Enables a previously disabled command in this channel.

ping.name: !ping
ping.description: Checks if the bot is responsive. Responds with Pong!

responseVariables.title: Response Variables
responseVariables.intro1: When you create a custom command, you can use response variables to insert dynamic content. Write them in your response using the $(variable) format and they'll be replaced automatically when the command is used.

user.desc: Name of the person who used the command

args.desc: All text after the command name as one string
args.example: If someone types !shout hello world, $(args) = hello world

numbered.desc: A single word by position — $(1) is the 1st word, $(2) is the 2nd, and so on
numbered.example: If someone types !shout hello world, $(1) = hello, $(2) = world

count.desc: How many times this command has been used

random.desc: A random number between X and Y

uptime.desc: How long the stream has been live

followage.desc: How long the user has been following the channel

responseVariables.examplesIntro: Here are some example commands you can create:

live.command: !command add live We've been live for $(uptime) playing $(game)!
live.result: We've been live for 2h 30m playing Metroid Prime!

uses.command: !command add uses This command has been used $(count) times!
uses.result: This command has been used 42 times!

aiCommands.title: AI Commands
aiCommands.intro1: AI commands use a prompt instead of a fixed response. The bot sends the prompt to an AI model and returns a unique answer every time. You can use response variables in prompts too!
aiCommands.intro2: The easiest way to create AI commands is through the Web Dashboard. Click + Add, flip on AI Mode, and write your prompt. You can also create them in chat with !command addai <name> <prompt>.

vibe.prompt:
Name: vibe
Prompt: Tell $(user) what kind of vibe "$(args)" gives off in one sentence.
vibe.result: When someone types !vibe I just ate an entire pizza, the AI might reply: "WildcatGamer your message gives off deeply respectable goblin energy." — different every time!

hug.prompt:
Name: hug
Prompt: Write a short, funny hug message from $(user) to $(1).
hug.result: When someone types !hug WildcatStreamer, the AI might reply: "WildcatGamer wraps WildcatStreamer in a hug so warm it could melt a glacier."

fortune.prompt:
Name: fortune
Prompt: Give $(user) a short fortune cookie message.
fortune.result: A unique fortune every time: "WildcatGamer, today you will find a rare item in an unexpected place."

aiCommands.tipText: You can convert any existing text command to AI mode by editing it in the dashboard and enabling AI Mode, or in chat with !command options <name> type=prompt. Switch back with type=text.

webDashboard.title: Web Dashboard
webDashboard.intro: The WildcatSage Web Dashboard provides streamers with a comprehensive interface to manage the bot in their channel. Access it by logging in with your Twitch account.

botManagement.title: Bot Management:
botManagement.desc: Add or remove WildcatSage from your channel

channelConfig.title: Channel Configuration:
channelConfig.desc: Configure auto-chat settings and toggle ad notifications

commandControl.title: Command Control:
commandControl.desc: Create and manage custom commands, and enable/disable built-in commands

authorize.title: Authorize:
authorize.desc: Click "Login with Twitch" and grant the required permissions

addBot.title: Add the Bot:
addBot.desc: Use the "Add Bot to My Channel" button to invite WildcatSage to your channel

configure.title: Configure:
configure.desc: Customize settings through the dashboard interface

grantPerms.title: Troubleshooting:
grantPerms.desc: If the bot isn't responding, type /mod WildcatSage in your Twitch chat to grant moderator status

webDashboard.oauthIntro: The dashboard requires the following Twitch OAuth scopes:

oauth.readEmail: Access your email address for account identification
oauth.readAds: Receive ad break notifications for your channel
oauth.manageModerators: Automatically grant the bot moderator status
oauth.readFollowers: Access follower information for commands like !followage

webDashboard.noteText: WildcatSage is currently invite-only. If you don't see your channel in the dashboard, please request access through the contact form.
